title: Breonia sphaerantha
intro: Breonia sphaerantha, also known as Madagascar king of hearts or Madagascar heartleaf, is a plant species native to the island of Madagascar, belonging to the family Rubiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been found to provide relief from various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Breonia sphaerantha include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Breonia sphaerantha is made into a tea or an infusion, which is also used in some modern herbal preparations, including capsules and tinctures, to leverage its health benefits.
taxonomy: Breonia sphaerantha is classified as a species within the plant family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the coffee or madder family. This family comprises approximately 600 genera and 13,000 species. Breonia sphaerantha is further classified as a member of the genus Breonia, which consists of around 20 species. The genus Breonia is native to tropical regions of Africa and Madagascar. The classification of Breonia sphaerantha reflects its evolutionary history and relationships with other plant species within the Rubiaceae family.
